title: dryas octopetala subsp. viscida
intro: Dryas octopetala subsp. viscida, also known as mountain avens, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the rose family and is native to mountainous regions in Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its saponins and flavonoids. The health benefits of mountain avens include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and acting as an antiseptic, making it useful for treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Mountain avens can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
